definition:
1. to make to rattle , of horses, ὄχεα κροτέοντες rattling  them along , (Iliad by Homer) 
2. to knock, strike, smite , (Herdotus Historicus); κροτεῖν τὰς χεῖρας or τὼ χεῖρε to clap  the hands, (Herdotus Historicus): absolute to clap, applaud , (Xenophon Historicus), etc. 
3. of a smith, to hammer  or weld together , (Plato Philosophus):—;Pass. to be wrought by the hammer ; metaphorically, ἐξ ἀπάτας κεκροταμένος one mass  of trickery, (Theocritus Poeta Bucolicus) 
4. intransitive to make a rattling noise , (Aristotle Philosopher) (ML)
